pi_items.source: task.json description step 1 explicitly states: 'Create a new survey titled "Employee Exit Interview 2024"'. Also verified in export_result.sh line 18 and verifier.py line 47.
task.json description step 3 explicitly states: 'set its Question Code explicitly to "EMPNAME"'. Also verified in export_result.sh line 38 and verifier.py line 63.
task.json description step 4 explicitly states: 'with Question Code "REASON"'. Also verified in export_result.sh line 45 and verifier.py line 80.

task.json description step 5 contains the piping question: 'Can you provide more details about why "{REASON.shown}" was the primary factor, {EMPNAME}?'. Also verified in export_result.sh line 65 and verifier.py line 96.

task.json description step 5 contains the piping question with the .shown suffix: 'Can you provide more details about why "{REASON.shown}" was the primary factor, {EMPNAME}?'. The description explicitly notes: 'You must use the .shown suffix to display the answer text.' Also verified in export_result.sh line 65, line 76 and verifier.py line 97. The .shown suffix is a valid LimeSurvey Expression Script syntax confirmed via LimeSurvey Manual at https://www.limesurvey.org/manual/ExpressionScript_examples

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a LimeSurvey survey titled 'Employee Exit Interview 2024' with Expression Script piping. The survey must include: (1) a mandatory Short Free Text question with code 'EMPNAME' asking for full name, (2) a List Radio question with code 'REASON' with 4 specific answer options ('Found a better opportunity', 'Salary/Benefits', 'Relocation', 'Career Change'), and (3) a Long Free Text question using piping syntax containing both '{EMPNAME}' and '{REASON.shown}' (with the .shown suffix to display answer text). The survey must be activated after creation. All metadata claims—the survey title, question codes, and piping syntax—are explicitly specified in the task description and verified across the setup scripts, export script, and verifier logic. The .shown suffix is a valid LimeSurvey Expression Script feature for displaying answer text.
